author: Mads Kristensen
releaseNotes:
v0.7.0: Quality pass. Faster polls (parse emoji rules once per poll, single-pass iCal unfolder, cheap base64 sentinel check instead of regex over the whole 1+ MB body). Bug fixes (older missed occurrences are now all marked triggered so they stop re-firing every poll; exact-trigger runIn overwrites instead of stacking; synthetic UIDs no longer depend on parse-order index; iCal text unescape ordering fixed; URL scheme guard; safer response.getStatus() handling). Internal: unified ISO timestamps to hub zone, logd helper, dead code removed.
v0.6.1: Idle icon/text now refresh whenever preferences are saved (was only seeded on first install). Default idle text changed to "Clear".
v0.6.0: New `idleEmoji` and `idleText` preferences. `todo` / `nextTodo` publish the idle text when there is no active or upcoming chore. `todoIcon` idle defaults to ✔️ but can be changed.
v0.4.0: Per-todo emoji mapping. New `emojiMap` preference (one rule per line, e.g. `🗑️ = trash, garbage`) chooses a category icon. Whole-word case-insensitive match, first matching line wins, configurable fallback emoji (default 🔔).
v0.3.6: Fix NPE in RRULE INTERVAL/COUNT parsing when those params are absent (Elvis default returned the literal but the ternary re-read the null source).
v0.3.5: Detect and base64-decode the response body. Hubitat base64-encodes response bodies whose Content-Type it doesn't recognize as text — Google serves text/calendar.
v0.3.4: Drop `textParser: true` so `response.getData()` returns the body as a String directly.
v0.3.3: Use Hubitat's built-in `now()` instead of `System.currentTimeMillis()` (sandbox-blocked).
v0.3.2: Replaced `instanceof Reader` body decode (blocked by the Hubitat sandbox) with a duck-typed read.
v0.3.1: Removed unused `java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit` import (blocked by the Hubitat sandbox).
v0.3.0: Replaced ContactSensor with Switch capability.
v0.2.0: RRULE expansion (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/YEARLY with INTERVAL, COUNT, UNTIL, BYDAY, EXDATE). Default prefix changed to ✅.
v0.1.0: Initial release.
